page: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, storage-optimized server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.8 GHz, with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it bundles 45,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, complemented by a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 10% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 18,388.9 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 403.47 GB/sec. Database and web serving capabilities are exceptionally strong, as demonstrated by top-tier results in Passmark database operations, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations, which reached 347.98 million operations per second.

While multi-threaded and memory-bound operations excel, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling in the middle 50% tier. Additionally, the server is poorly suited for LLM text generation, yielding weak to poor token generation speeds on 135M and 7B models, though prompt processing remains strong. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of DDR5 memory and massive 45 TB local NVMe storage bundled on a bare-metal ARM platform provides a highly resource-dense profile for large-scale deployments without hyperthreading overhead.

This server is optimized for high-throughput database systems, large-scale Redis deployments, static web serving, and heavy multi-threaded data processing tasks such as software compilation and decompression.
description: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a massive 45,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations achieving 347.98 million operations per second. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are relatively moderate. This hardware configuration is highly cost-efficient for resource-dense, multi-threaded workloads, making it an excellent fit for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and intensive data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i8g.metal-48xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 ARMv9 Graviton cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 45 TB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, storage-optimized ARM platform featuring massive local NVMe capacity and high-throughput multi-core performance for demanding database workloads.

45,000 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory delivering top-tier bandwidth
Top-tier performance in database operations and Redis throughput
Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and data compilation
